Mental Health Awareness Month: Supporting Well-being

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Victoria Creamer
3w
  • Report this post

Mental Health Awareness Month in the U.S. and Mental Health Awareness Week in the U.K. are important reminders that supporting well-being must be an ongoing commitment.   Creating environments where people feel supported, connected, and empowered to prioritize their well-being helps individuals and teams thrive. From emotional and social well-being to career and financial support, meaningful resources and open conversations can make a lasting impact.   This month and beyond, let’s continue encouraging conversations around mental health and reminding one another that seeking support is a sign of strength. #MentalHealthAwarenessMonth #MentalHealthAwarenessWeek

    Mental Health Awareness Month reminds us how vital it is to create supportive environments at work, at home, and in our communities. At Padagis, we recognize that employee well-being is key to thriving together. One of the most meaningful ways we can support mental well-being is by continuing to normalize the conversations around mental health. When people feel seen, heard, and understood, it can create a lasting impact. As Mental Health Awareness Month comes to a close, we recognize that supporting mental health is not limited to a single month — it is an ongoing commitment we strive to uphold every day. To anyone facing challenges, or supporting someone who is, please remember: you are not alone. Every conversation, every check-in, and every act of compassion matters. #MentalHealthAwarenessMonth #MentalHealthMatters

      This year’s Mental Health Awareness Week theme is action, and it feels like an important one. Because while awareness matters, awareness on its own does not always change what people are experiencing day to day. In our first video of the week, we’re talking about what action can really mean when it comes to mental health. Not just big gestures or perfect solutions, but the practical things that help create change. Across this week, we’ll be sharing short conversations exploring each of the three Mental Health awareness week action areas (self, someone else, for all of us), including reflections from our own community. We hope it opens up a thoughtful conversation about what meaningful action can look like, personally, collectively and structurally. #MentalHealthAwarenessWeek #MentalHealth #WorkplaceWellbeing #TheSteadyGroundCo #MentalHealthMatters

      • The Better People

        403 followers

        4w
        • Report this post

        It's Mental Health Awareness Week. The theme this year is Action - and for good reason, because awareness alone isn't enough. 91% of UK adults experienced high or extreme stress in the last year. 1 in 5 took time off sick because of it. And yet only 1 in 4 employees say mental health is genuinely prioritised where they work. The gap isn't awareness. It's action. This week, and every week, we're helping organisations move from good intentions to real change. Because your people deserve more than a poster in the break room. What action is your organisation taking this Mental Health Awareness Week? Tell us in the comments. #MentalHealthAwarenessWeek #MHAW2026 #Action #WorkplaceWellbeing #OrganisationalResilience #Leadership #PeopleFirst #TheBetterPeople

        This Mental Health Awareness Week, we’re highlighting the importance of taking time to pause and reset. 💚 We asked our team what they do to de-stress and unwind — and their answers show there’s no one-size-fits-all approach! From getting outdoors to relaxing in front of the TV, it’s the small habits that make a big difference. We’re proud to support a culture where mental health is valued and openly discussed. What helps you switch off and recharge? #mentalhealthawarenessweek #mentalhealthmatters #wellbeing

        • Michele Montgomery

          Mental Health Awareness Month may be coming to a close, but prioritizing your mental well-being is a year-round commitment. Your mental health is the foundation for both personal and professional growth. Don’t be afraid to: • Take that bucket-list trip (my husband and I did to Bora Bora) • Apply for the job you think you’re not qualified for • Read the book you’ve been wanting to start • Reconnect with a family member you haven’t spoken to in a while • Mend a relationship with a coworker • Do the things that bring you peace, joy, and a renewed sense of purpose Let’s continue to raise awareness, foster compassion, and support one another—not just during Mental Health Awareness Month, but every day. #MentalHealthAwarenessMonth #MentalHealthMatters #WellBeing #SelfCare #PersonalGrowth #ProfessionalGrowth #SupportOneAnother
